page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8y.large is an arm64-based server instance belonging to the ecs.r8y family. It is powered by a dedicated Alibaba YiTian710 processor running at 2.75 GHz, featuring 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor, includes a 64 MB L3 cache, and supports AES cryptography instructions, though it lacks advanced vector or matrix extensions. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. However, multi-core and parallelized workloads show limitations; the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 203.35 GB/sec, but uncached large-block write speeds drop to 13.63 GB/sec, which is in the bottom 10% of tested servers.

Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers a memory-optimized profile suitable for workloads requiring higher memory capacity relative to compute power, such as lightweight database operations, caching layers, and text processing. However, its weak multi-core performance, poor ray tracing, and low encryption scores make it less suitable for compute-intensive, heavily parallelized, or cryptographic workloads. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the custom Arm-based YiTian710 architecture provides a dense memory allocation per core, making it a viable option for memory-bound tasks that do not require high-throughput floating-point or vector calculations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8y.large is an arm64-based, memory-optimized server instance powered by a dedicated 2.75 GHz Alibaba YiTian710 processor. It features 2 physical cores, 2 vCPUs, and 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, as seen in Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tests, but multi-core and memory write benchmarks fall into the weak and poor categories. This resource profile is best suited for memory-bound workloads with low compute intensity, such as lightweight database operations, caching, and text processing, where high memory density per vCPU is prioritized over raw parallel processing power.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8y.large is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring a 2.75 GHz YiTian710 processor, 2 vCPUs, and 16 GB RAM, suitable for memory-bound workloads with moderate single-core compute needs.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8y.large is an arm64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 2.75 GHz YiTian710 CPU, designed for memory-optimized workloads.
tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring high memory density per core for lightweight database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Alibaba YiTian710 arm64 processor running at 2.75 GHz
Features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core)
Delivers average single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core scaling
Includes 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Best suited for lightweight databases, caching, and text processing tasks
